active_ingredients: Cephalexin 250 mg, 500 mg, 750 mg capsules/tablets; 125 mg/5 mL and 250 mg/5 mL oral suspensions (e.g., Keflex) - prescription only
indications_and_usage: Indicated for the treatment of respiratory tract infections, otitis media, skin and skin-structure infections, bone infections, and genitourinary tract infections caused by susceptible organisms. It is a first-generation cephalosporin (beta-lactam) antibiotic. Prescription only.
contraindications: Known severe hypersensitivity to cephalosporins or to any of the inactive ingredients. Cross-reactivity with penicillins is possible (low but real, especially with first-generation cephalosporins) - use caution if the patient had a non-severe penicillin allergy, and avoid in penicillin-anaphylaxis history without specialist input.
warnings_and_cautions: Anaphylaxis and serious hypersensitivity reactions, including Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, and drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS). Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea. Direct Coombs' test can become positive during therapy. Patients with renal impairment need a dose adjustment. Use caution in seizure disorders (rare CNS toxicity with very high doses). Prolonged use may result in overgrowth of nonsusceptible organisms.
adverse_reactions: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, headache, rash; rarely anaphylaxis, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, DRESS, C. difficile colitis, interstitial nephritis, transient blood-count abnormalities.
drug_interactions: Probenecid (raises levels); metformin (cephalexin can modestly raise metformin levels); warfarin (rare reports of altered INR); no oral contraceptive interaction of clinical significance.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children >=15 years: 250-500 mg orally every 6 hours, or 500 mg every 12 hours, depending on the indication (e.g., uncomplicated cystitis - 500 mg every 12 hours or 250 mg every 6 hours; skin/soft tissue - 500 mg every 6-12 hours). Children: 25-50 mg/kg/day in divided doses (every 6-12 hours), or up to 100 mg/kg/day for severe infections, max 4 g/day. Reduce dose in renal impairment. Take with or without food; complete the full course.
overdosage: Overdose may cause nausea, vomiting, epigastric distress, diarrhea, and hematuria; serious toxicity is unusual. Hemodialysis can remove cephalexin. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
